Defines the CrumbsLayer structure and functions for initialization, creation, and animation handling.

# Purpose
This header file defines the `CrumbsLayer` structure and declares functions for managing and manipulating instances of this structure. The `CrumbsLayer` structure includes a `Layer` object and properties for level, background color, foreground color, and an internal increment value for x-coordinates. The functions declared in this file allow for the initialization, creation, setting of level, deinitialization, and destruction of `CrumbsLayer` objects. Additionally, the file provides a function to retrieve an animation associated with a `CrumbsLayer` and a function to get the width of the crumbs layer. The file includes dependencies on other modules related to animation, layer management, and graphics.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/ui/animation.h`
- `applib/ui/layer.h`
- `applib/graphics/graphics.h`


# Data Structures

---
### CrumbsLayer
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``layer``: A `Layer` object that represents the base layer of the `CrumbsLayer`.
    - ``level``: An integer that indicates the current level of the `CrumbsLayer`.
    - ``bg_color``: A `GColor` object that specifies the background color of the `CrumbsLayer`.
    - ``fg_color``: A `GColor` object that specifies the foreground color of the `CrumbsLayer`.
    - ``crumbs_x_increment``: An internal `int16_t` value used for incrementing the x-coordinate of crumbs.
- **Description**: Defines a graphical layer structure that includes a base layer, level indicator, and color settings for both background and foreground, with an internal mechanism for x-coordinate incrementation of crumbs.

### crumbs\_layer\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:crumbs_layer_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/crumbs_layer.h#L32>)

Initializes a `CrumbsLayer` with specified frame and colors.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize a `CrumbsLayer` structure before using it in your application. This function sets up the layer with a given frame and assigns foreground and background colors. It also initializes the layer's level to zero and sets the appropriate update procedure based on the display type. Call this function before using any other operations on the `CrumbsLayer`. Ensure that the `crumbs_layer` pointer is valid and points to a properly allocated `CrumbsLayer` structure.
- **Inputs**:
    - `crumbs_layer`: A pointer to a `CrumbsLayer` structure that must be allocated by the caller. Must not be null.
    - `frame`: A pointer to a `GRect` structure that defines the frame of the layer. Must not be null.
    - `bg_color`: A `GColor` value that specifies the background color of the layer.
    - `fg_color`: A `GColor` value that specifies the foreground color of the layer.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`crumbs_layer_init`](<crumbs_layer.c.md#crumbs_layer_init>)  (Implementation)

### crumbs\_layer\_create<!-- {{#callable_declaration:crumbs_layer_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/crumbs_layer.h#L35>)

Creates a new `CrumbsLayer` with specified frame and colors.
- **Description**: Use this function to create a new `CrumbsLayer` object with a specified frame size and background and foreground colors. This function allocates memory for the `CrumbsLayer` and initializes it with the given parameters. Ensure to call `crumbs_layer_destroy` to free the allocated memory when the `CrumbsLayer` is no longer needed. This function returns a pointer to the newly created `CrumbsLayer`, or `NULL` if memory allocation fails.
- **Inputs**:
    - `frame`: Defines the size and position of the `CrumbsLayer`. Must be a valid `GRect` structure.
    - `fg_color`: Specifies the foreground color of the `CrumbsLayer`. Must be a valid `GColor` value.
    - `bg_color`: Specifies the background color of the `CrumbsLayer`. Must be a valid `GColor` value.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the newly created `CrumbsLayer`, or `NULL` if memory allocation fails.
- **See Also**: [`crumbs_layer_create`](<crumbs_layer.c.md#crumbs_layer_create>)  (Implementation)

### crumbs\_layer\_set\_level<!-- {{#callable_declaration:crumbs_layer_set_level}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/crumbs_layer.h#L37>)

Sets the level of a CrumbsLayer.
- **Description**: Use this function to set the current level of a `CrumbsLayer` object. This function updates the level and marks the layer as dirty, which triggers a redraw. If the specified level exceeds the maximum allowed, the function clamps the level to the maximum and logs a warning. Ensure that the `CrumbsLayer` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `crumbs_layer`: A pointer to a `CrumbsLayer` object. Must not be null. The function modifies the level of this object.
    - `level`: An integer representing the desired level. If this value exceeds the maximum allowed, it is clamped to the maximum.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`crumbs_layer_set_level`](<crumbs_layer.c.md#crumbs_layer_set_level>)  (Implementation)


---

Ensure that the `crumbs_layer` parameter is not null before calling this function, as passing a null pointer will result in no operation.
- **Inputs**:
    - `crumbs_layer`: A pointer to a `CrumbsLayer` object that you want to deinitialize. Must not be null. If null, the function does nothing.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`crumbs_layer_deinit`](<crumbs_layer.c.md#crumbs_layer_deinit>)  (Implementation)

### crumbs\_layer\_destroy<!-- {{#callable_declaration:crumbs_layer_destroy}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/crumbs_layer.h#L41>)

Releases resources associated with a `CrumbsLayer`.
- **Description**: Use this function to release all resources associated with a `CrumbsLayer` object when it is no longer needed. This function must be called to prevent memory leaks after a `CrumbsLayer` is created and used. Ensure that the `crumbs_layer` parameter is not null before calling this function. After calling this function, the `CrumbsLayer` object is no longer valid and should not be used.
- **Inputs**:
    - `crumbs_layer`: A pointer to a `CrumbsLayer` object. Must not be null. The function will release resources associated with this object.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`crumbs_layer_destroy`](<crumbs_layer.c.md#crumbs_layer_destroy>)  (Implementation)

### crumbs\_layer\_get\_animation<!-- {{#callable_declaration:crumbs_layer_get_animation}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/crumbs_layer.h#L43>)

Retrieves an animation for a given crumbs layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain an animation associated with a `CrumbsLayer` object. This function is useful when you need to animate the properties of a `CrumbsLayer`. Ensure that the `CrumbsLayer` is properly initialized before calling this function. The function returns an `Animation` object that can be used to control the animation lifecycle, such as starting or stopping the animation.
- **Inputs**:
    - `crumbs_layer`: A pointer to a `CrumbsLayer` object. Must not be null. The `CrumbsLayer` should be initialized before calling this function. The caller retains ownership of the `CrumbsLayer`.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to an `Animation` object associated with the given `CrumbsLayer`. The caller does not own the returned `Animation` object and should not attempt to free it.
- **See Also**: [`crumbs_layer_get_animation`](<crumbs_layer.c.md#crumbs_layer_get_animation>)  (Implementation)

### crumbs\_layer\_width<!-- {{#callable_declaration:crumbs_layer_width}} -->

Returns the width of the crumbs layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the current width of the crumbs layer. This function does not require any parameters and can be called at any time to retrieve the width value. It is useful for layout calculations or when you need to know the dimensions of the crumbs layer for rendering purposes.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns an integer representing the width of the crumbs layer.
- **See Also**: [`crumbs_layer_width`](<crumbs_layer.c.md#crumbs_layer_width>)  (Implementation)
